# GitHub Workflows

This directory contains GitHub Actions workflows, including a mix of
hand-written YAML workflows and workflows generated from
[GitHub Agentic Workflows](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/) (`gh-aw`)
agentic workflow definitions.

## Agentic workflows (gh-aw)

Some workflows in this directory are **autogenerated** from agentic
workflow definitions written in Markdown. Do not edit the generated
files directly — edit the source `.md` file and regenerate.

### Source and generated files

| Source (edit this) | Generated (do not edit) | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `doc-bot.md` | `doc-bot.lock.yml` | Documentation bot that analyzes PRs and proposes documentation updates in `vaadin/docs`. |
| _(none — generated automatically)_ | `agentics-maintenance.yml` | Scheduled maintenance job that closes expired discussions, issues, and pull requests created by agentic workflows. Regenerated whenever any agentic workflow uses the `expires` field on a safe-output. |

Pinned action versions and SHAs used by the generated workflows are
tracked in [`../aw/actions-lock.json`](../aw/actions-lock.json).

### When regeneration happens

The generated files are regenerated whenever:

- An agentic workflow `.md` file in this directory is edited.
- The `gh-aw` CLI is upgraded and the pinned action versions change.
- A safe-output's `expires` field is added, removed, or changed (this
  affects `agentics-maintenance.yml` and its schedule).

### How to regenerate

Install the `gh-aw` extension once:

```bash
gh extension install github/gh-aw
```

Then, from the repository root, run:

```bash
gh aw compile
```

This reads the agentic workflow `.md` files, refreshes
`.github/aw/actions-lock.json`, and regenerates the corresponding
`*.lock.yml` workflows plus `agentics-maintenance.yml`.

Commit the regenerated files together with the source `.md` changes.

### Further reading

- Agentic workflows documentation: https://github.github.com/gh-aw/
- `gh-aw` CLI: https://github.com/github/gh-aw
